title: Meet The Scientist BANNED By TED Talks (ft. Rupert Sheldrake)
summary: Jesse Michels interviews Rupert Sheldrake, a controversial scientist known for his work on morphic fields, the sense of being stared at, and consciousness studies. They discuss Sheldrake's theories about vision as projection, electromagnetic fields as interfaces for consciousness, the potential consciousness of the sun, and his hypothesis of morphic resonance as a mechanism for non-genetic inheritance.

acronyms.expansion: from 'scop' (seeing, as in microscope) and 'aesthesia' (feeling, as in anesthesia)
acronyms.definition: The scientific term for the sense of being stared at

events.description: Rupert Sheldrake's TED Talk was censored and removed from YouTube
9/11 World Trade Center disaster; Sheldrake collected precognitive dream accounts related to the event
Carrington Event - a major solar storm that affected electrical systems

key_claims.claim: Visual images are projected outward from the mind to where objects appear to be, rather than being constructed entirely inside the brain
The transactional interpretation of quantum mechanics suggests that when light is absorbed by the eye, an anti-photon is sent backward in time to the source
The sense of being stared at (scopaesthesia) evolved in connection with predator-prey relations and operates directionally
Precognitive dreams about 9/11 were collected, falling into three categories: planes flying into skyscrapers, being trapped in blazing buildings, and walking through dust-filled streets
The sun may be conscious, with its electromagnetic field serving as the interface between its consciousness and physical body
If a frog embryo is placed in a Faraday cage or next to a Wi-Fi router, it will not grow normally
DNA and genes cannot account for the inheritance of complex organizational patterns like the structure of a termite colony or animal instincts
Most scientists privately hold open-minded views about psychic phenomena but are afraid to discuss them publicly due to career concerns
